title: FDA Launches Real-Time Clinical Trial Data Pilot with AstraZeneca and Amgen
description: On April 28, the FDA announced two proof-of-concept real-time clinical trials (RTCTs), allowing its reviewers to access safety and efficacy signals as they are generated rather than waiting for full data submissions. AstraZeneca's Phase 2 TRAVERSE trial in mantle cell lymphoma and Amgen's STREAM-SCLC Phase 1b trial in small-cell lung carcinoma are already sharing live data through the Paradigm Health platform. This structural shift in trial oversight could compress approval timelines by months and represents the most significant regulatory modernization in drug development in years, with a broader summer 2026 pilot program to follow.

title: D+Q Senolytic Combo Causes Brain Myelin Damage in Mice, Raising Human Safety Flags
description: A study published in PNAS and covered by Lifespan.io on April 28 found that dasatinib and quercetin, the most widely used senolytic combination currently in multiple clinical trials, causes demyelination of the corpus callosum in both young and old mice, mimicking multiple sclerosis-like white matter injury. Oligodendrocytes were not killed but lost their ability to properly deliver and organize myelin via endoplasmic reticulum stress within 20 minutes of exposure. This finding is a major safety signal for the millions following D+Q longevity protocols and may prompt regulatory scrutiny of ongoing human trials testing this combination.

title: UT Southwestern Identifies Refeeding Phase, Not Fasting, as Key to Longevity Extension
description: Research from UT Southwestern published in Nature Communications on April 26 found that the lifespan-extending effect of intermittent fasting derives from the metabolic recalibration during refeeding rather than from caloric deprivation itself. The protein NHR-49 must be efficiently deactivated by the enzyme KIN-19 when food is reintroduced; keeping NHR-49 continuously active eliminated the 60% lifespan extension in C. elegans models. This mechanistic insight reframes intermittent fasting protocol design and identifies a druggable switch (NHR-49/KIN-19) that could deliver fasting benefits without dietary restriction.

title: Fragmented Circadian Rhythms Linked to Accelerated Brain Shrinkage in Aging Adults
description: A longitudinal Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health study published in Alzheimer's & Dementia followed 344 cognitively healthy adults aged 50 and older and found that those with more fragmented daily rest-activity rhythms experienced significantly faster shrinkage of the hippocampus, parahippocampus, and amygdala — regions first targeted by Alzheimer's disease — than those with stronger circadian patterns. The association was strongest among the oldest participants, suggesting circadian fragmentation may both cause and be caused by neurodegeneration in a compounding cycle. This positions wearable-measured circadian regularity as an early modifiable biomarker for Alzheimer's risk stratification.

title: Dual Senolytic-Epigenetic Strategy Defined as Next-Generation Healthspan Framework
description: A comprehensive review in MDPI Biomolecules, highlighted in the April 28 Healthcare Discovery AI brief, argues that combining epigenetic modulation with senolytic therapy produces a more powerful anti-aging framework than either approach alone, targeting both the DNA methylation and histone modification signatures of senescent cells and the cells themselves. The review catalogs major senolytic classes including BCL-2 inhibitors, tyrosine kinase inhibitors, and natural polyphenols alongside senomorphic agents that modulate SASP signaling, and projects that AI-driven molecular profiling paired with epigenetic aging clocks will define the next generation of precision senotherapy. This synthesis arrives as the D+Q safety signal (above) further motivates the field to develop more tissue-specific and combinatorial approaches.

title: FDA Real-Time Clinical Trial Pilot Announced with AstraZeneca and Amgen
description: The FDA unveiled its real-time clinical trial initiative, with AstraZeneca's TRAVERSE Phase 2 lymphoma trial and Amgen's STREAM-SCLC Phase 1b already transmitting live data via Paradigm Health. The agency also released a Request for Information for a broader summer 2026 AI-optimization pilot.

title: A Popular Senolytic Treatment Causes Brain Damage in Mice
description: Lifespan.io reported on a new PNAS study showing that the D+Q senolytic combo reduces myelination in the corpus callosum of mice, stressing oligodendrocytes via endoplasmic reticulum stress. The damage resembled multiple sclerosis and appeared in both young and old mice.

title: Fasting Extends Life by 60% But the Secret Is in the Refeeding
description: Nature Communications published UT Southwestern research showing that the NHR-49/KIN-19 metabolic switch during refeeding is the critical determinant of fasting-induced lifespan extension in C. elegans, with over 60% lifespan gains eliminated when NHR-49 remained active through the refeeding window.

title: Chronic Insomnia Raises Dementia Risk by 40%, Six-Year Longitudinal Study Finds
description: A six-year study of more than 2,700 cognitively healthy adults found that chronic insomniacs were 40% more likely to develop mild cognitive impairment or dementia, with greater amyloid burden and white matter hyperintensities on brain imaging, reinforcing sleep disruption as a primary modifiable driver of neurodegeneration.

title: Nasal Spray Using Neural Stem Cell Vesicles Reverses Brain Aging in Preclinical Study
description: Texas A&M researchers published in the Journal of Extracellular Vesicles that just two doses of an intranasal spray delivering extracellular vesicles from human neural stem cells dramatically reduced hippocampal neuroinflammation, restored mitochondrial function, and improved memory in aged mice by suppressing NLRP3 inflammasome and cGAS-STING pathways. The non-invasive delivery bypasses the blood-brain barrier.

title: Harvard Identifies Gut Bacteria-Pollution Pathway Triggering Inflammation Linked to Depression
description: Harvard and Broad Institute researchers publishing in JACS found that Morganella morganii interacts with the common pollutant diethanolamine to produce a modified phospholipid that activates TLR1/TLR2 receptors and drives IL-6-mediated inflammation, providing a mechanistic gut-to-brain pathway for certain cases of major depressive disorder.

title: SuperAgers Grow 2.5x More New Neurons Than Age-Matched Peers
description: A Nature study from UIC and Northwestern found that adults over 80 classified as SuperAgers generate two to two-and-a-half times more new hippocampal neurons than healthy age-matched peers, establishing neurogenesis as the central 'resilience signature' of exceptional cognitive aging and a potential target for brain longevity therapies.

title: 60 Minutes of Weekly Resistance Training Identified as Optimal Longevity Dose in 111,000-Person Harvard Study
description: A Harvard study tracking 111,000 adults for 30 years found approximately 60 minutes per week of resistance exercise associated with a 19% lower risk of premature death, with exercise variety consistently outperforming single exercise modalities, supporting a revision of clinical exercise guidelines for older adults.

title: Muscle Power Beats Muscle Strength as the Top All-Cause Mortality Predictor
description: A Mayo Clinic Proceedings prospective study found that relative muscle power — the ability to generate force quickly — is a significantly stronger predictor of all-cause mortality than relative muscle strength alone in middle-aged and older adults, arguing for the inclusion of power training in longevity exercise prescriptions.

title: Women with Highest Grip Strength Show 33% Lower Mortality Risk in 5,000-Person Study
description: A study following more than 5,000 women aged 63-99 found that those in the highest grip strength category had a 33% lower risk of death, with fastest chair-stand times associated with a 37% reduction, even after controlling for aerobic fitness and general activity levels, supporting grip strength as a standard mortality risk stratification tool.

title: Centenarian Offspring Eat More Fish, Fruit, and Dramatically Less Sodium, Tufts 20-Year Study Finds
description: A Tufts University study using 20 years of data from the New England Centenarian Study found that offspring of parents who lived past 100 consistently ate more fish, fruits, and vegetables alongside substantially lower sodium and added sugar versus age-matched peers, identifying diet as a controllable non-genetic longevity pathway.

title: Fragmented Circadian Rhythms Linked to Faster Brain Shrinkage in Older Adults
description: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health researchers following 344 cognitively healthy adults aged 50+ found that weaker, more fragmented daily rest-activity rhythms predicted faster hippocampal, parahippocampal, and amygdala atrophy, establishing circadian fragmentation as an early modifiable Alzheimer's neurodegeneration biomarker.

title: eBooks Delay Melatonin Onset by 90 Minutes Compared to Paper Books
description: A study published April 27 in Clocks & Sleep found that reading e-books delayed melatonin onset by approximately 90 minutes and sleep timing by 10 minutes versus paper books under matched lighting conditions, strengthening clinical recommendations for screen-free pre-bed intervals in longevity sleep protocols.

title: Tirzepatide Delivers Significant Benefit for Hard-to-Treat HFpEF Heart Failure
description: An international NEJM study found that tirzepatide, the dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist, significantly reduced symptoms and improved exercise capacity in patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, potentially establishing GLP-1 agents as a cornerstone of a form of heart failure that affects roughly half of all heart failure patients and has historically lacked effective therapies.

title: Metabolic Acidosis Identified as Potential Contributing Cause of Age-Related Frailty
description: The Fight Aging! newsletter April 27 edition reviewed new research linking low serum bicarbonate (below 25 mEq/L) even with preserved renal function to impaired gait, muscle strength, functional limitation, and mortality, implicating acidosis-driven sarcopenia via catabolic signaling, insulin resistance, and mitochondrial dysfunction as an underappreciated frailty driver.

title: FGF8 Gene Therapy Enhances Digit Tip Regrowth in Mice, Pointing Toward Limb Regeneration
description: Researchers using an AAV vector to deliver spatiotemporally guided FGF8 expression via a zebrafish enhancer rescued and accelerated digit tip regrowth in mice by targeting the SP6/SP8 transcription factor program, identified as conserved across vertebrates, offering a pathway toward mammalian limb regeneration strategies.

title: Mitrix Bio Reports Phase 1 Safety Data for Autologous Mitochondrial Infusions in Older Adults
description: Mitrix Bio reported that large-volume autologous mitochondrial infusions in two older participants at Right to Try clinics in Dallas and Newport Beach showed no adverse effects on blood chemistry or physical monitoring at escalating doses, with bioreactor-produced autologous mitochondria and efficacy data expected by year-end 2026.

title: Nature Aging Review Clarifies Dietary Restriction Mechanisms Across Species
description: A comprehensive Nature Aging review by 16 leading aging researchers synthesized how dietary restriction extends lifespan via reduced insulin/IGF-1 signaling, enhanced autophagy, improved mitochondrial function, and gut microbiome remodeling, noting that human translation requires accounting for caloric source, timing, protein composition, and metabolic variation beyond what animal studies have addressed.

title: Gut Microbiome Signature Can Predict Parkinson's Disease Risk, UCL Study Finds
description: A UCL analysis found that gut microbial composition analysis can identify elevated Parkinson's disease risk, with people carrying Parkinson's-associated genetic variants displaying the same distinctive microbiome profile as those with confirmed diagnoses, opening gut-based screening and intervention avenues for a neurodegenerative disease with aging as its primary risk factor.

title: Google Research: Wearables Plus Routine Blood Tests Can Predict Insulin Resistance Without Invasive Testing
description: A Google Research study developed and validated machine learning models combining wearable data (resting heart rate, steps, sleep) with standard blood tests to predict insulin resistance in 1,165 participants, particularly in obesity and sedentary individuals, presenting a scalable non-invasive metabolic aging monitoring approach for longevity medicine practice.

title: Fasting Mimicking Diet Triggers Measurable Autophagy in First Human Clinical Trial
description: A Cedars-Sinai and UT Health San Antonio randomized pilot trial found that the five-day ProLon Fasting Mimicking Diet produced a measurable increase in autophagic flux alongside improvements in body weight, fasting glucose, insulin sensitivity, and ketone levels in 30 healthy participants, representing the first human confirmation of autophagy activation through nutritional intervention.

title: Longevity Aging Reframed as Loss of Biological Coordination at Berlin World Congress
description: Researchers presenting at the 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin outlined a conceptual shift from viewing aging as accumulated cellular defects toward understanding it as a progressive loss of coordination among biological systems including mitochondrial signaling, microbiota-brain interactions, and metabolic tissue regulation, pointing toward systems biology and comparative genomics as the most productive future research directions.

title: Tau Protein Spreads Through the Brain via Synaptic Connections, Not Diffusion
description: University of Alabama at Birmingham researchers publishing in Neuron found that Alzheimer's-associated tau tangles spread between brain regions by traveling along synaptic connections rather than diffusing freely through tissue, a mechanistic finding that reframes therapeutic targeting strategies for tau-based neurodegeneration interventions.

title: NIH All of Us Comprehensive Wearables Dataset Released in Nature Medicine
description: The NIH All of Us Research Program released a comprehensive wearables dataset in Nature Medicine providing physiological and behavioral data from commercial devices across a large diverse national cohort, creating a foundational resource for aging biomarker discovery and longitudinal healthspan research.

title: Insilico Medicine Forms Industry-First Longevity Board for AI-Driven Aging Research
description: Insilico Medicine announced the formation of the industry's first dedicated Longevity Board to provide scientific oversight for AI-enabled aging research and drug discovery, overseeing life models, biomarkers of aging, dual-purpose longevity targets, and clinical validation of therapeutics targeting hallmarks of aging in combination with its $2.75B Eli Lilly R&D partnership.
